title: Formidable Red Sox extend win streak to 11 games with doubleheader sweep of Rays
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox extended their win streak to 11 games after sweeping the Tampa Bay Rays in a doubleheader.
In Game 1, the Red Sox defeated the Rays 10-0 with a strong performance by starting pitcher Jake Bennett.
Game 2 highlighted Wilyer Abreu's two home runs as the Red Sox secured a 5-3 victory and reached a .500 season record.

title: Chad Tracy offers updates on several injured Red Sox pitchers
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Ranger Suarez and Connelly Early are progressing well; Suarez could return to start on July 21 after a groin strain.
Kutter Crawford resumes long-distance throwing; Tanner Houck, recovering from Tommy John surgery, throws from a mound twice a week.
Roman Anthony, Garrett Crochet, and others remain shut down with no return timetable due to ongoing injuries.

title: 4 Red Sox who rose their stock following Friday’s doubleheader sweep vs. Rays
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Masataka Yoshida excelled with three hits, including a home run and RBI, bolstering his standing in the Red Sox lineup.
Caleb Durbin delivered a solid performance, going 5 for 7 with an RBI and two runs in the doubleheader.
Jake Bennett pitched 6 scoreless innings, benefiting the bullpen with only one hit and one walk allowed.

title: Robert Kraft backs Mike Vrabel after controversial offseason: ‘No one is infallible’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Robert Kraft expressed support for Patriots coach Mike Vrabel after offseason controversies and denied affair rumors.
Mike Vrabel missed part of the NFL draft amid personal issues; Dianna Russini resigned from The Athletic in April.
Vrabel led the Patriots to the Super Bowl last year after being appointed head coach following Bill Belichick's departure.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Anya Taylor-Joy stars in 'Lucky,' a seven-episode series streaming on Apple TV about a family crime saga.
'Obsession,' a horror film directed by Curry Barker, grossed over $400 million and streams on Peacock.
'The Hunt for the Wilderpeople,' featuring the late Sam Neill, is available on Prime Video and Plex.

title: NH judge: Concord man violated Civil Rights Act in assault on transgender woman
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A NH judge ruled Travis Lufkin violated the Civil Rights Act in a 2024 assault on a transgender woman.
Lufkin was fined $5,000, with $4,000 suspended, and ordered to avoid contact with the victim for three years.
Lufkin received a total 24-month sentence for assault, with portions of it suspended for compliance with court orders.

title: Red Sox’ trade deadline plans ‘fluid,’ ESPN’s Jeff Passan says
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox's trade deadline strategy remains fluid as they consider options before the August 3 deadline.
The team's recent performance, including a 10-game winning streak, has improved their playoff odds to 44.3% per FanGraphs.
ESPN reports the Red Sox are not dismissing acquiring help, though trading closer Aroldis Chapman is unlikely according to MLB insiders.

title: Popular Nantucket pizzeria Pi is for sale for $12.5 million
author: Madison Lucchesi
bullets: Pi Pizzeria in Nantucket, owned by Evan and Maria Marley, is for sale for $12.5 million after 25 years.
The sale includes a 46-seat dining room, takeout area, staff housing, and a wood fire oven on West Creek Road.
Owners seek a buyer committed to continuing the brand; Evan Marley plans to ensure a smooth ownership transition.

title: France’s World Cup squad bids adieu to Boston
author: John Waller
bullets: The France national soccer team departed Boston after over a month, moving out of the Four Seasons Hotel.
France will face England in Miami for the third-place match after losing to Spain 2-0 in the semifinals.
While in Boston, the French team trained at Bentley University and used the entire Four Seasons Hotel for lodging.

title: Matt Damon says he’s working on a Karen Read movie
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Matt Damon announced his production company, Artists Equity, is developing a movie about Karen Read's murder case.
Karen Read was acquitted of murder charges in the 2022 death of her boyfriend, Boston Police Officer John O’Keefe.
Several adaptations, including a Lifetime film and a Netflix documentary, are also exploring the Karen Read case.
